name: boarderless-mcp
description: Connect Claude to your live Boarderless spatial canvas: inspect, create, edit, group, undo, and export objects on the human-visible board.
long_description:
Boarderless is a local-first spatial canvas at https://boarderless.app. This extension runs the Boarderless MCP server on your machine and attaches to your own signed-in, human-visible canvas tab via the Chrome DevTools Protocol (port 9222). Claude can then read the board's typed spatial ledger, create and mutate text and shapes, group/ungroup, reorder, undo/redo, export PNG/PDF/SVG, and maintain durable `.bdrl.json` board files in a workspace folder you choose.

**Local-first boundary:** the browser owns the working canvas. This server never uploads your board to a hidden cloud copy, never inherits your Google identity, and only writes board files inside the configured workspace directory.

**Requirements:** Windows 10/11 (primary; macOS/Linux community fallback), a Chromium browser (Chrome, Edge, Brave, Opera) launched with remote debugging on port 9222, and a signed-in session at https://boarderless.app/canvas. The server can auto-launch the browser if the port is closed. Run the `get_server_status` tool first — it diagnoses browser, auth, and bridge state with step-by-step fixes.

tools.name: get_server_status
tools.description: Returns a full diagnostic status report for the Boarderless MCP server. Use this first to verify connectivity, authentication, and tool availability before attempting canvas operations. Includes brows

tools.name: get_board_state
tools.description: Read the current Boarderless board as a flat JSON spatial ledger.

tools.name: mutate_object
tools.description: Patch one Boarderless Object by id and route the mutation to its owning Zustand store.

tools.name: remix_style
tools.description: Apply a canonical Boarderless palette to selected shape/text Objects or the whole board as one undoable Style Remix.

tools.name: calculate_export_bounds
tools.description: Calculate the absolute mathematical export bounding box for the current composition.

tools.name: create_object
tools.description: Create a new object (text or shape: rect, ellipse, triangle, arrow) on the canvas.

tools.name: delete_objects
tools.description: Delete one or more objects by their IDs from the canvas.

tools.name: history_undo
tools.description: Undo the last action on the canvas.

tools.name: history_redo
tools.description: Redo the next action in the history queue on the canvas.

tools.name: group_objects
tools.description: Group multiple canvas objects under a unique groupId.

tools.name: ungroup_objects
tools.description: Ungroup objects belonging to a specific groupId.

tools.name: reorder_object
tools.description: Reorder z-index layering of an object (bring to front, send to back, etc.).

tools.name: get_board_workspace
tools.description: Return the local workspace directory used for automatic schema-v2 .bdrl.json snapshots after canvas mutations.

tools.name: set_board_workspace
tools.description: Set the absolute local project directory used for automatic and explicit .bdrl.json board files.

tools.name: export_board_file
tools.description: Atomically save the current canonical Boarderless board inside the configured workspace as .bdrl.json.

tools.name: import_board_file
tools.description: Import and open a schema-v2 .bdrl.json file from the configured local workspace.

tools.name: export_board
tools.description: Export the current Boarderless canvas to PNG, PDF, or SVG. Requires the user to be authenticated. SVG and PDF require a Pro plan.

tools.name: graduation_rename_photos
tools.description: Rename and number photo files inside a directory to a sequential format (e.g. seniorname_01.jpg). Operates on local filesystem.

tools.name: graduation_standardize_images
tools.description: Scan and convert progressive JPEGs and HEIC files inside subdirectories into standard baseline RGB JPEGs. Operates on local filesystem.

workspace_dir.title: Board workspace folder
workspace_dir.description: Folder where canonical .bdrl.json board files are saved after every successful canvas change.

app_url.title: Boarderless canvas URL
app_url.description: The Boarderless canvas page to attach to.

browser_url.title: Browser debugging address
browser_url.description: Visible Chromium remote-debugging endpoint the server attaches to; headless and invisible sessions are rejected.
